Glossary
Anisotropic Having direction-dependent properties.
Arcade-like structure
A collagen fibre arrangement in articular cartilage whereby fibres curve from th
Beaded microfibrillar network A structural arrangement of thin, bead-like fibril
Fibrillogenesis The process of collagen fibril formation.
Heterotypic fibres Collagen fibres composed of more than one type of collagen mo
Hypertrophy The process by which cells undergo significant enlargement owing to
Isotropic Having uniform properties in all directions.
Joint cavitation The formation of the joint cavity during embryonic development,
Liquid crystallinity A state of matter with properties between those of a liquid
Molecular crowding
A high concentration of macromolecules in a confined space that influences molec
Non-reducible crosslinks A non-reversible chemical bond that permanently stabili
Nucleator
A molecule or structure that promotes the initiation of fibrillogenesis.
Procollagen
The precursor molecule of collagen that undergoes enzymatic processing to form t
Reducible crosslinks A reversible chemical bond that stabilizes collagen fibrils
Resistance to the positive principal strain The ability of a material to withsta

Boundary between the calcified and non-calcified zones of articular cartilage.

The basic triple-helical collagen molecule that assembles into fibrils.
